channels.label: Maharashtra Chief Minister Helpline / Citizen Call Centre (Aaple Sarkar)
channels.source_name: Aaple Sarkar - Government of Maharashtra (official state portal)
channels.note: Published on the state's Aaple Sarkar portal with the explicit label "Chief Minister Helpline (Toll Free)", hours "12*7 (8AM to 8PM)", for any information/service/scheme related to State Govt departments. The Aaple Sarkar grievance portal separately publishes the same number as "Helpline : 1800 120 8040 (Toll Free)" and describes a "24 X 7 Citizen Call Center" - the two official pages disagree on hours, so treat 8AM-8PM as the safer stated window.

channels.label: Aaple Sarkar - Grievance Redressal Portal (file & track a grievance)
channels.source_name: Aaple Sarkar Grievance Redressal Portal - Government of Maharashtra
channels.note: Maharashtra's official online public grievance portal. Run by the Chief Minister's Office, Mantralaya, Mumbai, supported by the Commissionerate of Electronics, IT and AI. Issues a token number on submission for status tracking.

channels.label: Maharashtra Government - official state portal
channels.source_name: Government of Maharashtra - Official Website
channels.note: Official Government of Maharashtra website (महाराष्ट्र शासन). Verified live.

channels.label: Maharashtra District Portal - directory of all 36 district websites
channels.source_name: Maharashtra | District Portal (NIC S3WaaS)
channels.note: NIC/S3WaaS-hosted directory page linking to each district's own official website (Ahilyanagar, Akola, Amravati, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Beed, Bhandara, Buldhana, Chandrapur, Dhule, Gadchiroli, Gondia, Hingoli, Jalgaon, Jalna, Kolhapur, Latur, Mumbai City, Mumbai Suburban, Nagpur, Nanded, Nandurbar, Nashik, Dharashiv, Palghar, Parbhani, Pune, Raigad, Ratnagiri, Sangli, Satara, Sindhudurg, Solapur, Thane, Wardha, Washim, Yavatmal). Note district sites sit on mixed domains (*.gov.in, *.nic.in, *.maharashtra.gov.in).

channels.label: Maharashtra Police - Citizen Services Portal (CCTNS)
channels.source_name: Maharashtra Police - Services for Citizen (CCTNS)
channels.note: Official Maharashtra Police citizen portal on the Crime and Criminal Tracking Network and Systems (CCTNS) platform. Offers e-Complaint registration, missing person search, published FIR access, arrested persons and absconders databases, and festival mandal registration.

channels.label: Maharashtra State Police - official website
channels.source_name: Maharashtra State Police - Official Website
channels.note: Official Maharashtra State Police site; publishes the state's emergency and helpline short codes on its homepage.

channels.label: Dial 112 Citizen Portal - Maharashtra Emergency Response System
channels.source_name: Maharashtra State Police - Official Website (links to Dial 112 Citizen Portal)
channels.note: Maharashtra Police emergency response citizen portal, linked from the official mahapolice.gov.in site. For emergencies/incidents requiring immediate intervention; also supports missing person and stolen vehicle reports.

channels.label: Women's Safety Helpline - Mumbai, Thane & Navi Mumbai
channels.source_name: Maharashtra State Police - Official Website
channels.note: State-specific split published on the Maharashtra Police homepage under "महिला सुरक्षा" (Women's Safety): "१०३ (मुंबई, ठाणे आणि नवी मुंबई), १०९१ (उर्वरित महाराष्ट्र)". Use 103 within Mumbai, Thane and Navi Mumbai only.

channels.label: Women's Safety Helpline - rest of Maharashtra
channels.source_name: Maharashtra State Police - Official Website
channels.note: Published on the Maharashtra Police homepage under "महिला सुरक्षा": "१०३ (मुंबई, ठाणे आणि नवी मुंबई), १०९१ (उर्वरित महाराष्ट्र)" - 1091 applies outside Mumbai/Thane/Navi Mumbai.

channels.label: Maharashtra Police Women's Safety - direct line
channels.source_name: Maharashtra State Police - Contact Us
channels.note: Published under "महिला सुरक्षा" on both the Maharashtra Police homepage and its Contact Us page as "८९७६००४१११, ८६५७२२२७७७, ०२२४५१६१६३५". The two mobile numbers 8976004111 and 8657222777 are published alongside it.

channels.label: Maharashtra Emergency Medical Service (ambulance)
channels.source_name: Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra - Helpline
channels.note: Published by the Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra as "Emergency Medical Service: 108". Also appears on the Maharashtra Police homepage as "महाराष्ट्र आणीबाणी वैद्यकीय सेवा - १०८".

channels.label: Health Advice, Blood Availability, Grievance Redressal & Mental Health Counselling
channels.source_name: Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra - Helpline
channels.note: Published by the Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra as "Health Advice, Blood Availability, Grievance Redressal, Mental Health Counseling: 104".

channels.label: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana (state health scheme helpline)
channels.source_name: Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra - Helpline
channels.note: State-specific health scheme helpline published by the Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra.

channels.label: Mental Health Rehabilitation Counselling (Tele-MANAS)
channels.source_name: Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra - Helpline
channels.note: Published by the Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra as "Mental Health Rehabilitation Counseling: 14416".

channels.label: MSEDCL / Mahavitaran electricity complaints - Maharashtra outside Mumbai city & suburbs
channels.source_name: MSEDCL (Mahavitaran) - Contact Us (official DISCOM site)
channels.note: MSEDCL (Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Co. Ltd) publishes "1912, 19120, 1800-233-3435, 1800-212-3435" as its centralised customer care lines. MSEDCL covers most of Maharashtra but NOT all of Mumbai - parts of Mumbai are served by BEST, Adani Electricity and Tata Power, which have their own numbers.

channels.label: MSEDCL / Mahavitaran - electricity complaints (alternate toll-free)
channels.source_name: MSEDCL (Mahavitaran) - Contact Us (official DISCOM site)
channels.note: One of four centralised customer care numbers published by MSEDCL: "1912, 19120, 1800-233-3435, 1800-212-3435". The alternate 1800-212-3435 and the short code 19120 are published alongside it.

channels.label: MSEDCL / Mahavitaran - consumer contact & complaint registration
channels.source_name: MSEDCL (Mahavitaran) - Contact Us (official DISCOM site)
channels.note: Official MSEDCL contact page. Also publishes customercare@mahadiscom.in, an HT consumer helpdesk (022-26478989 / 022-26478899), an online payment helpdesk (022-26478246), and a missed-call power-failure registration line (022-50897100).

channels.label: MSEDCL centralised customer care / consumer helpdesk email
channels.source_name: MSEDCL (Mahavitaran) - Contact Us

channels.label: Adani Electricity 24x7 helpline - Mumbai suburbs
channels.source_name: Adani Electricity Mumbai - Contact Us (official site)
channels.note: Printed as '19122 (24x7)' on the official contact page; WhatsApp 9594519122 for supply complaints and bills, email helpdesk.mumbaielectricity@adani.com. Serves the Mumbai suburban distribution licence area.

channels.label: Adani Electricity Mumbai consumer helpdesk email
channels.source_name: Adani Electricity - Contact Us

channels.label: Tata Power Mumbai consumer helpline - Mumbai licence area (Colaba to Mira-Bhayander)
channels.source_name: Tata Power - Mumbai distribution (official page)
channels.note: Published on Tata Power's Mumbai distribution page along with toll-free 1800-209-5161 and the customer portal customerportal.tatapower.com. Mumbai licence area of 485 sq km from Colaba to Mira-Bhayander.

channels.label: Tata Power customer care email (Mumbai distribution)
channels.source_name: Tata Power - Mumbai Distribution

channels.label: BEST electricity complaints (South Mumbai) - Mumbai island city
channels.source_name: Brihanmumbai Electric Supply & Transport Undertaking - Contact Us
channels.note: BEST supplies electricity in Mumbai's island city. Complaint numbers published: South Mumbai 022-22085888 / 022-22082875, North (of the island city) 022-24145888 / 022-24114891; email electric@bestundertaking.com.

channels.label: National Emergency Number (ERSS) - Police, Fire & Rescue, Health
channels.source_name: Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
channels.note: MHA's single pan-India emergency number: 'ONE INDIA ONE EMERGENCY NUMBER 112', a nationwide unified emergency response system. Works in every State/UT. Corroborated by india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 112 as the Integrated Helpline (ERSS) covering Police, Fire & Rescue and Health services.

channels.label: Fire Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed under 'Emergency Numbers' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: National Ambulance Service (NAS)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Ambulance Service (NAS)' under Emergency Numbers on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Women Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Women Helpline Toll free No. - 181' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed on india.gov.in as both 'Women Helpline Number' and 'Domestic Violence Helpline'.

channels.label: National Commission for Women (NCW) 24x7 Helpline
channels.source_name: National Commission for Women (NCW)
channels.note: NCW's own site states: 'Call the 24x7 NCW helpline at 14490 for immediate assistance and support', and lists 'NCW Women Helpline (14490)' under its Complaint Redressal cells. IMPORTANT: the india.gov.in helpline directory still lists 7827170170 as the 'National Commission for Women Helpline' - that number appears nowhere on ncw.gov.in (not even in raw HTML), so it appears stale and was deliberately omitted in favour of the department's own current number.

channels.label: Child Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Child Helpline Toll free No. - 1098' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed as 'Child Help Line' on india.gov.in.

channels.label: Elderline - National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)
channels.source_name: Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Elder Helpline: 14567' on the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment's own Elderline site, which the ministry homepage links to as the 'National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)'. india.gov.in additionally lists a landline 011-20893999 alongside 14567 for the Senior Citizens Helpline.

channels.label: National Consumer Helpline (NCH)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Consumer Helpline (NCH)' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026). Note: the NCH portal consumerhelpline.gov.in could not be reached at time of research (DNS resolves to an NIC address but the host refused connections / returned 504), so only the number is cited here - not the NCH portal URL.

channels.label: Cyber Crime Helpline (online financial fraud reporting)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Confirmed on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal homepage banner (MHA / Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre - I4C), which reads: 'To report online financial fraud, call 1930' and 'register your complaint at cybercrime.gov.in'. Independently corroborated by the india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 1930 as the Cyber Crime Helpline.

channels.label: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (file a cybercrime complaint)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Verified live. Ministry of Home Affairs portal for reporting cybercrime; supports anonymous reporting, women/children-related crime, financial fraud and other cybercrime, plus complaint tracking. Nationwide.

channels.label: CPGRAMS - Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System
channels.source_name: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Government of India
channels.note: Verified live; this is the citizen-facing portal. The site states CPGRAMS is 'an online platform available to the citizens 24x7 to lodge their grievances to the public authorities on any subject related to service delivery... a single portal connected to all the Ministries/Departments of Government of India and States.' Supports tracking by registration ID and an appeal facility. Note: it does not cover RTI matters, subjudice/court matters, or religious matters.

channels.label: Central Vigilance Commission - lodge a corruption complaint
channels.source_name: Central Vigilance Commission (CVC), Government of India
channels.note: Verified live. CVC's Complaint Management System with a 'Citizen Login', complaint status tracking and a published complaint handling policy. Linked from cvc.gov.in as 'Lodge a complaint'. Covers corruption complaints against central government organisations.

channels.label: Relief Commissioner for Natural Calamities
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Road Accident Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).
